description: The `sui::display` module's modification functions (`update_version`, `add`, `add_multiple`, `edit`, `remove`) for `Display<T>` objects lack proper authorization checks. While `Display<T>` creation correctly requires a `&Publisher` capability, these modification functions only take a mutable reference to the `Display<T>` object. This allows any owner of a `Display<T>` instance, obtained via `create_and_keep`, to bypass the intended `Publisher` authorization. Consequently, unauthorized parties can alter the display rules for a type `T`, contradicting the module's stated access control policy and potentially leading to misrepresentation or manipulation of asset metadata.
remediation: Modify all `Display<T>` object modification functions (`update_version`, `add`, `add_multiple`, `edit`, `remove`) to require a `&Publisher` capability as an argument, similar to the creation functions. This ensures that only the authorized publisher can alter the display rules for a given type `T`.
